programme.title: Sailing Deejay - Episode 5
programme.desc: Today we are going to visit the Hippie Market at Formentera, famous all over the world for its unique type of creativity. We will be making new friends that will be teaching us about their favourite music styles and, this close to the end, will our two protagonists be getting sentimental? Not without a mojito at the beach first! Let's dive in!

programme.title: Sailing Deejay - Episode 6
programme.desc: Our adventure is coming to an end, but the party never stops! Sam and Max have lived their adventure in Ibiza and Formentera, and have to get back home to show family and friends their new skills. We are sailing one last time and partying until dawn to make the most out of our time in Ibiza.

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to Sailing to Tokyo 2020! We begin this first episode by travelling to Sorento in Melbourne, Australia, where the 2020 RS:X Windsurfing World Championships took place. A fleet of 116 sailors from 31 nations gathered in Sorento Sailing Couta Boat Club to challenge for the prestigious titles. Later on, we examine how the Coronavirus pandemic affected an athlete's routine. With the majority of the countries instituting some sort of stay-at-home order, public gatherings of any kind were banned, and numerous sport events were postponed and cancelled.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 5 - Return
programme.desc: After nearly two months at sea, which now seems like lifetimes compounded by the suffering and challenge of the passage. The three are closing out their trip home and saving the best for last. Shocked by what seems to be death island, the three uncover shark hunters and in their last village before departure battle to resupply with the essentials of gas and water with the last pennies on the boat. Meanwhile the struggles of our filmmaker’s journey return along with the fear of going back to all that was left behind.

programme.title: The World Sailing Show - Episode 12
programme.desc: The World Sailing Show - Episode 12

programme.title: Qatar Airways GKA Kite World Tour Magazines - Episode 3
programme.desc: Qatar Airways GKA Kite World Tour Magazines - Episode 3

programme.title: Transat Bretagne - Martinique
programme.desc: Fifteen sailors, 3500 miles and one epic race. The Transat Bretagne-Martinique departs from the French coast and sailors travel in solitary across the Atlantic Ocean in the monohulls class Figaro Bénéteau. With eight rookies in the line-up and veterans that know the perils of the journey, this race will prove to be a hard endurance test on the participants.The course is long and treacherous, and for over 2 weeks, these athletes will have to fight against harsh conditions to reach the Caribbean destination. Will the young passion from the rookies really be enough to trump hard-earned experience?Find out the outcome of the Transat Bretagne – Martinique, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Inside Kiel - Sailing Capital and more
programme.desc: Kiel is the nautical epicentre of Germany, and the proud host of the world's biggest sailing event, the Kieler Woche. From learning how to sail a Formula18 catamaran to exploring local kitesurf spots and shipyards, Nautical Channel host Lucia Metzbauer will be there, diving into the local nautical scene and finding out why there are so many young sailors in Kiel.Join her in Kieler Woche, one of the largest sailing events in the world. With regattas sailing at the highest level and an unbeatable class diversity, entertainment is secured as we learn everything about this German event that drives the attention of sailors worldwide.

programme.title: Rolex World Sailing Awards - Episode 1
programme.desc: 2021 has marked a great year in the nautical world. With the Olympics, America's Cup and Vendee Globe, it is great sailing is back and strong. Let's look back on the past year and find out who of this year's nominees will become World Sailor of the Year as well as what sustainable project has been received the World Sailing 11th Hour Racing Sustainability Award.

programme.title: A Day At DIBS
programme.desc: Dubai International Boat Show, the most trusted and established marine industry exhibition in the Middle East. Despite misconceptions claiming luxury yachts to be the main attraction in this event, the DIBS offer a much wider variety of expositions on nautical news, sports and lifestyle. It differs from other shows as it has a more exclusive clientele and many professionals of this world are yearning to come.You will adventure into one of Dubai’s most prestigious shows featuring the latest releases of yachts and boats from both local and international builders together with the innovations in marine equipment and accessories.Discover all its secret gems with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 2
programme.desc: Welcome to The Boat Show, the program dedicated to nautica, sports and excitement. In today's episode we will discover the Mercury Diesel 6.7L, built by FB Design. Then we will get onboard the Lomac Adrenalina 10.5 before trying out the Master 996 con 2x350 cv Suzuki. Dive in with us!

programme.title: AF Offshore
programme.desc: AF Offshore

programme.title: Sails In Sardinia
programme.desc: Sails in Sardinia takes us to the beautiful Italian island of Sardinia, the sescond largest island in the Mediterranean Sea, to visit the Yacht Club Costa Smeralda, in Porto Cervo. Known for being visited by the rich and famous, it also has a unique sailing history. We will get the true essence of competitive sailing at the Audi Settimana Delle Bocche, where the last leg of the TP52 Super Series is being held. We are meeting Ricardo Bonadeo, Commodore of the yacht club, to understand better how the 1967 non-profit organization has become today a world's leading organizer in international sailing events. Join Andi Van Zyl as she heads to the beautiful Costa Smeralda and interviews other big sailing personalities as Francesco Bruni and Terry Hutchinson, on Sails in Sardinia.

programme.title: Jump On Board - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to our new adventure of Jump on Board! We are with Abi and our captain Marcos, and we will be sailing the Ionian Islands with the new girls: Cindy, Caitlin and Linda. The islands are known for their pristine clear blue waters, strong wind and unspoiled nature. Are you jumping on board with us?

programme.title: Jump On Board - Episode 2
programme.desc: In this episode of Jump on Board, the girls and I will sail from Corfu to Paxos, the island created by Poseidon according to Greek mythology when he struck his trident on Corfu, breaking it in two. Let's hope that Linda, Cindy and Caitlin don't divide like the islands as they get to know each other better. Jump on board with us to find out!

It differs from other shows as it has a more exclusive clientele and many professionals of this world are yearning to come.You will adventure into one of Dubai’s most prestigious shows featuring the latest releases of yachts and boats from both local and international builders together with the innovations in marine equipment and accessories.Discover all its secret gems with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 2
programme.desc: Welcome to The Boat Show, the program dedicated to nautica, sports and excitement. In today's episode we will discover the Mercury Diesel 6.7L, built by FB Design. Then we will get onboard the Lomac Adrenalina 10.5 before trying out the Master 996 con 2x350 cv Suzuki. Dive in with us!

programme.desc: AF Offshore

programme.title: Sails In Sardinia
programme.desc: Sails in Sardinia takes us to the beautiful Italian island of Sardinia, the sescond largest island in the Mediterranean Sea, to visit the Yacht Club Costa Smeralda, in Porto Cervo. Known for being visited by the rich and famous, it also has a unique sailing history. We will get the true essence of competitive sailing at the Audi Settimana Delle Bocche, where the last leg of the TP52 Super Series is being held. We are meeting Ricardo Bonadeo, Commodore of the yacht club, to understand better how the 1967 non-profit organization has become today a world's leading organizer in international sailing events. Join Andi Van Zyl as she heads to the beautiful Costa Smeralda and interviews other big sailing personalities as Francesco Bruni and Terry Hutchinson, on Sails in Sardinia.

programme.title: Ice Canoe Carnival Quebec
programme.desc: Rowing through icy currents in the cold is not for the faint of heart. However, every year competitors jump into their canoes to race in the Ice Canoe Carnival Quebec. The Canoe Race is a legendary competition that has been held since the Carnival's first edition. When the Québec Winter Carnival returned in 1955, ice canoe racing became once again an important annual event, and is now awaited by many in the Canadian city.Each year, several courageous teams compete in extremely freezing conditions a tumultuous ride along the St. Lawrence River between Quebec City and Levis. Find out which team will win against the cold and icy Canadian conditions!

programme.title: Bora Bora: Paddling Paradise
programme.desc: In Bora Bora: Paddling Paradise Nikolai visits the crystal blue lagoons, encounters sharks and stingrays and witnesses the 6th edition of the Muramur surfski race in Tahiti. The intensive 38 km race meandering through reefs that surround the mystic island of Bora Bora is bound to make you pack your flippers and book a flight to the exotic French Polynesia.You can now watch this exclusive show that will take you to one of the world’s most beautiful sceneries on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Speed Catamarans GC32 - Episode 4
programme.desc: We arrive to Spain for the GC32 Mar Menor Cup! The crews are ready for a grandiose final of the season as this is their last chance to improve their standings. Precision, coordination and pure skill is of essence, and Spanish waters will prove to be harder to navigate than expected. Will Allinghi take the overall victory?Watch the best moments of the GC32 Mar Menor Cup 2021.

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to Sailing to Tokyo 2020! We begin this first episode by travelling to Sorento in Melbourne, Australia, where the 2020 RS:X Windsurfing World Championships took place. A fleet of 116 sailors from 31 nations gathered in Sorento Sailing Couta Boat Club to challenge for the prestigious titles. Later on, we examine how the Coronavirus pandemic affected an athlete's routine. With the majority of the countries instituting some sort of stay-at-home order, public gatherings of any kind were banned, and numerous sport events were postponed and cancelled.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 5 - Return
programme.desc: After nearly two months at sea, which now seems like lifetimes compounded by the suffering and challenge of the passage. The three are closing out their trip home and saving the best for last. Shocked by what seems to be death island, the three uncover shark hunters and in their last village before departure battle to resupply with the essentials of gas and water with the last pennies on the boat. Meanwhile the struggles of our filmmaker’s journey return along with the fear of going back to all that was left behind.

programme.title: Qatar Airways GKA Kite World Tour Magazines - Episode 3
programme.desc: Qatar Airways GKA Kite World Tour Magazines - Episode 3

programme.title: Transat Bretagne - Martinique
programme.desc: Fifteen sailors, 3500 miles and one epic race. The Transat Bretagne-Martinique departs from the French coast and sailors travel in solitary across the Atlantic Ocean in the monohulls class Figaro Bénéteau. With eight rookies in the line-up and veterans that know the perils of the journey, this race will prove to be a hard endurance test on the participants.The course is long and treacherous, and for over 2 weeks, these athletes will have to fight against harsh conditions to reach the Caribbean destination. Will the young passion from the rookies really be enough to trump hard-earned experience?Find out the outcome of the Transat Bretagne – Martinique, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Skeleton Sea
programme.desc: João and Xandi, two surfers in their early 50′s, have been working together for 25 years. They clean beaches and with the trash they pick they create the most stunning artwork. However, living as an artist and idealist in Portugal is not economically profitable and, therefore, not easy.Their family life is affected by the choices they make and their wish to raise awareness about the ever-growing pollution in our oceans. Skeleton Sea takes us on their personal journey to help people understand the importance of our oceans to help us take the step to change our habits to keep them clean. As all live comes from the sea, Skeleton Sea reminds us that this powerful force has the ability of taking it away from us as well.

programme.title: Sylt - The Queen Of The North Sea
programme.desc: Welcome to Sylt! The northernmost island of Germany spreads for 40 kilometers and has been since ages a top nautical location. All year round you can find action here at the North Sea, and Nautical Channel host Lucia Metzbauer is ready to disembark in this location and explore the queen of the North Sea with our team. She will meet the locals, get to know the hidden gems, the best places for surfing and sailing, discover the wildlife and explore the enormous PWA Grand Slam in Sylt – the windsurfing event to be! Get ready to dive into Sylt's secrets as we wander around an UNESCO World Heritage Site and get splashed with the windsurf action. Let's get going to Sylt - The Queen of the North Sea!

programme.title: Surf Ski - Episode 1 - Perth
programme.desc: Welcome to Perth, Australia for the Surfski World Tour. The Perth Doctor is the second leg of the championship, and Jasper Mocké, the younger brother of four times Surfski World Series winner Dawid Mocke has already started strong in the Mandurrah Duel. Will he win again in Perth? Let's find out on Surf Ski!

programme.title: Surf Ski - Episode 2 - Mauritius
programme.desc: This time we are in Mauritius for the fifth leg of the Surf Ski World Tour. Athletes are getting hyped up for the Mauritius Ocean Classic, and many want to stop Jasper Mocké from winning as he has already won 3 of the 4 events disputed so far this season. Could Hank McGregor, winner of the Molokai Challenge, be his strongest challenger?

programme.title: Surf Ski - Episode 3 - Durban
programme.desc: Welcome to Durban! The Surf Ski World Tour is reaching its end, and standing at the pinnacle of the sport, the best open ocean paddlers from around the worlds reunite to take part in it. Jasper Mocké has proved to be in shape all season, but we have seen other important names raise to the top. Discover the exciting world of surf ski on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Sickline Extreme Kayak World Championship - Episode 1
programme.desc: 175 of the world's best kayakers are gathering for the 10th anniversary edition of the Sickline Extreme Kayak World Championship. They are committed to find the ideal or "sickest" line on this challenging competition. Racing up the creek, they are fighting for the world championship. Watch the best moments of the 2017 Adidas Sickline Extreme Kayak World Championship.

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to Sailing to Tokyo 2020! We begin this first episode by travelling to Sorento in Melbourne, Australia, where the 2020 RS:X Windsurfing World Championships took place. A fleet of 116 sailors from 31 nations gathered in Sorento Sailing Couta Boat Club to challenge for the prestigious titles. Later on, we examine how the Coronavirus pandemic affected an athlete's routine. With the majority of the countries instituting some sort of stay-at-home order, public gatherings of any kind were banned, and numerous sport events were postponed and cancelled.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 5 - Return
programme.desc: After nearly two months at sea, which now seems like lifetimes compounded by the suffering and challenge of the passage. The three are closing out their trip home and saving the best for last. Shocked by what seems to be death island, the three uncover shark hunters and in their last village before departure battle to resupply with the essentials of gas and water with the last pennies on the boat. Meanwhile the struggles of our filmmaker’s journey return along with the fear of going back to all that was left behind.

programme.title: Transat Bretagne - Martinique
programme.desc: Fifteen sailors, 3500 miles and one epic race. The Transat Bretagne-Martinique departs from the French coast and sailors travel in solitary across the Atlantic Ocean in the monohulls class Figaro Bénéteau. With eight rookies in the line-up and veterans that know the perils of the journey, this race will prove to be a hard endurance test on the participants.The course is long and treacherous, and for over 2 weeks, these athletes will have to fight against harsh conditions to reach the Caribbean destination. Will the young passion from the rookies really be enough to trump hard-earned experience?Find out the outcome of the Transat Bretagne – Martinique, on Nautical Channel.

Could Hank McGregor, winner of the Molokai Challenge, be his strongest challenger?

programme.title: The Women and the Waves - Part 2
programme.desc: The Women and the Waves 2 is a documentary surf film that celebrates women and their unique approach to surfing and life. The film looks at the state of surfing today from a feminine perspective and features positive role models that give back to people and the planet. Following in the footsteps of the groundbreaking 2009 film, The Women and The Waves Part 2 continues to explore the culture of surfing in relation to six surfers and their lives both in and out of the water.

Will the young passion from the rookies really be enough to trump hard-earned experience?Find out the outcome of the Transat Bretagne – Martinique, on Nautical Channel.

programme.desc: Welcome to Perth, Australia for the Surfski World Tour. programme.title: Surf Ski - Episode 2 - Mauritius
programme.desc: This time we are in Mauritius for the fifth leg of the Surf Ski World Tour. Athletes are getting hyped up for the Mauritius Ocean Classic, and many want to stop Jasper Mocké from winning as he has already won 3 of the 4 events disputed so far this season. Could Hank McGregor, winner of the Molokai Challenge, be his strongest challenger?

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to Sailing to Tokyo 2020! We begin this first episode by travelling to Sorento in Melbourne, Australia, where the 2020 RS:X Windsurfing World Championships took place. A fleet of 116 sailors from 31 nations gathered in Sorento Sailing Couta Boat Club to challenge for the prestigious titles. Later on, we examine how the Coronavirus pandemic affected an athlete's routine. With the majority of the countries instituting some sort of stay-at-home order, public gatherings of any kind were banned, and numerous sport events were postponed and cancelled.

programme.title: Reaching Reality - Episode 5 - Return
programme.desc: After nearly two months at sea, which now seems like lifetimes compounded by the suffering and challenge of the passage. The three are closing out their trip home and saving the best for last. Shocked by what seems to be death island, the three uncover shark hunters and in their last village before departure battle to resupply with the essentials of gas and water with the last pennies on the boat. Meanwhile the struggles of our filmmaker’s journey return along with the fear of going back to all that was left behind.

programme.title: Sails In Sardinia
programme.desc: Sails in Sardinia takes us to the beautiful Italian island of Sardinia, the sescond largest island in the Mediterranean Sea, to visit the Yacht Club Costa Smeralda, in Porto Cervo. Known for being visited by the rich and famous, it also has a unique sailing history. We will get the true essence of competitive sailing at the Audi Settimana Delle Bocche, where the last leg of the TP52 Super Series is being held. We are meeting Ricardo Bonadeo, Commodore of the yacht club, to understand better how the 1967 non-profit organization has become today a world's leading organizer in international sailing events. Join Andi Van Zyl as she heads to the beautiful Costa Smeralda and interviews other big sailing personalities as Francesco Bruni and Terry Hutchinson, on Sails in Sardinia.

programme.title: A Day At DIBS
programme.desc: Dubai International Boat Show, the most trusted and established marine industry exhibition in the Middle East. Despite misconceptions claiming luxury yachts to be the main attraction in this event, the DIBS offer a much wider variety of expositions on nautical news, sports and lifestyle. It differs from other shows as it has a more exclusive clientele and many professionals of this world are yearning to come.You will adventure into one of Dubai’s most prestigious shows featuring the latest releases of yachts and boats from both local and international builders together with the innovations in marine equipment and accessories.Discover all its secret gems with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 2
programme.desc: Welcome to The Boat Show, the program dedicated to nautica, sports and excitement. In today's episode we will discover the Mercury Diesel 6.7L, built by FB Design. Then we will get onboard the Lomac Adrenalina 10.5 before trying out the Master 996 con 2x350 cv Suzuki. Dive in with us!

programme.title: Sail For Surf
programme.desc: Collin Martins is a French chef with only one goal in mind: surfing. His passion started when he was just 3 years old. This was when his parents got him on a surfboard for the first time in Cap Ferret. It often starts like this. However, Collin’s course and his passion for surfing took a really atypical and unique turn.Looking for the best waves around the world, he decides to step into the unknown in 2017 and become autonomous living on a sailboat. From here he patiently studies the weather conditions, direction of the swell and tides to predict where the best waves will appear in order to surf them. Without any previous knowledge in the field, he carried out a professional training of marine navigation, bought a sailboat and took as headland Indonesia.Follow his exhilarating journey on his sailboat as he blends in Indonesian culture while catching the best waves.

programme.title: Untouched Waters In Turkmenistan
programme.desc: Welcome to the eastern coast of the Caspian sea. Turkmenistan, Avasa, is known for its desert and not so much for its nautical resort. Nonetheless, Turkmenistan is hosting the PWA World Cup 2014 right here. Nautical Channel host Nikolai Berger explores the untouched waters of the Caspian Sea to discover the first time ever of the PWA Windsurfing World Cup. How will the best windsurfers in the world handle this challenging and unexplored location? Will Antoine Albeau’s brute force triumph again? Find out if the Caspian Sea can become one of the new exciting locations, with just the right wind conditions for this world class in Untouched Waters in Turkmenistan. Only on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Vilanculos Downwind
programme.desc: We are in Mozambique, the southern African paradise with the long Indian Ocean coastline to its side. Mozambique is not only filled with popular beaches and offshore marine parks, but also incredible windsurfing spots. On this occasion, we celebrate Vilanculos Downwind, a never before done 20 kilometer kitesurf race from the mainland of Mozambique to Bazaruto Island.Join the windsurfers that will journey in the tricky Indian Ocean and race to reach the island of Bazaruto. Departing from the city of Vilanculos, they head for a journey downwind like no other. Don’t miss this once-in-a-lifetime experience, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Tiree Wave Classic - 2017
programme.desc: Thanks to the famed Atlantic swells that roll along its coastline and despite being home to just 650 people, this watersports mecca attracts surfers and windsurfers from across the world. Tiree's wild coastline is battered by some of the UK's highest wind ratings, creating the perfect meteorological conditions to host the longest running windsurfing event in the world - the Tiree Wave Classic. It is time for the 2017 edition. Come join us!

Athletes are getting hyped up for the Mauritius Ocean Classic, and many want to stop Jasper Mocké from winning as he has already won 3 of the 4 events disputed so far this season. Could Hank McGregor, winner of the Molokai Challenge, be his strongest challenger?

programme.title: Jump On Board - Episode 4
programme.desc: This time on Jump on Board, Cindy, Caitlin and Linda sail from Lefkada to Ithaca, home of Ulysses. The girls will discover the islands beauty, unspoiled by the modern world and the place where Penelope had the difficult task of fending off her suitors, alone, without her husband's help. Who will lose the challenge and be the next Penelope between our girls?

programme.title: Jump On Board - Episode 4
programme.desc: This time on Jump on Board, Cindy, Caitlin and Linda sail from Lefkada to Ithaca, home of Ulysses. The girls will discover the islands beauty, unspoiled by the modern world and the place where Penelope had the difficult task of fending off her suitors, alone, without her husband's help. Who will lose the challenge and be the next Penelope between our girls?

programme.title: Vilanculos Downwind
programme.desc: We are in Mozambique, the southern African paradise with the long Indian Ocean coastline to its side. Mozambique is not only filled with popular beaches and offshore marine parks, but also incredible windsurfing spots. On this occasion, we celebrate Vilanculos Downwind, a never before done 20 kilometer kitesurf race from the mainland of Mozambique to Bazaruto Island.Join the windsurfers that will journey in the tricky Indian Ocean and race to reach the island of Bazaruto. Departing from the city of Vilanculos, they head for a journey downwind like no other. Don’t miss this once-in-a-lifetime experience, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Tiree Wave Classic - 2017
programme.desc: Thanks to the famed Atlantic swells that roll along its coastline and despite being home to just 650 people, this watersports mecca attracts surfers and windsurfers from across the world. Tiree's wild coastline is battered by some of the UK's highest wind ratings, creating the perfect meteorological conditions to host the longest running windsurfing event in the world - the Tiree Wave Classic. It is time for the 2017 edition. Come join us!

programme.title: AF Offshore
programme.desc: AF Offshore

programme.title: Sails In Sardinia
programme.desc: Sails in Sardinia takes us to the beautiful Italian island of Sardinia, the sescond largest island in the Mediterranean Sea, to visit the Yacht Club Costa Smeralda, in Porto Cervo. Known for being visited by the rich and famous, it also has a unique sailing history. We will get the true essence of competitive sailing at the Audi Settimana Delle Bocche, where the last leg of the TP52 Super Series is being held. We are meeting Ricardo Bonadeo, Commodore of the yacht club, to understand better how the 1967 non-profit organization has become today a world's leading organizer in international sailing events. Join Andi Van Zyl as she heads to the beautiful Costa Smeralda and interviews other big sailing personalities as Francesco Bruni and Terry Hutchinson, on Sails in Sardinia.

programme.title: Sails In Sardinia

Join Andi Van Zyl as she heads to the beautiful Costa Smeralda and interviews other big sailing personalities as Francesco Bruni and Terry Hutchinson, on Sails in Sardinia.

programme.title: Jump On Board - Episode 1
programme.desc: Welcome to our new adventure of Jump on Board! We are with Abi and our captain Marcos, and we will be sailing the Ionian Islands with the new girls: Cindy, Caitlin and Linda. The islands are known for their pristine clear blue waters, strong wind and unspoiled nature. Are you jumping on board with us?

programme.title: Speed Catamarans GC32 - Episode 5
programme.desc: Throughout the season, the four stops offered nail-biting action in breath taking venues that brought the best out of these dynamic and fast design foiling catamarans. The level of expectation and the anticipation was extremely high and both the organizers and the participants proved that it was worth waiting for the comeback. Let’s remember the most notable moments of what was a fascinating season full of top-level sailing battles in some crystal clear blue waters.

programme.title: A Day At DIBS
programme.desc: Dubai International Boat Show, the most trusted and established marine industry exhibition in the Middle East. Despite misconceptions claiming luxury yachts to be the main attraction in this event, the DIBS offer a much wider variety of expositions on nautical news, sports and lifestyle. It differs from other shows as it has a more exclusive clientele and many professionals of this world are yearning to come.You will adventure into one of Dubai’s most prestigious shows featuring the latest releases of yachts and boats from both local and international builders together with the innovations in marine equipment and accessories.Discover all its secret gems with Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 2
programme.desc: Welcome to The Boat Show, the program dedicated to nautica, sports and excitement. In today's episode we will discover the Mercury Diesel 6.7L, built by FB Design. Then we will get onboard the Lomac Adrenalina 10.5 before trying out the Master 996 con 2x350 cv Suzuki. Dive in with us!

programme.title: San Borondón
programme.desc: The popular legend claims San Borondón is an island that appears and disappears, normally under a heavy veil of fog. As adventurers departed on missions to be the first to locate it, not all returned from the depths of the ocean.Now, San Borondón is the name many local and international surfers have given to a dangerous wave spot on the Canary Islands. The film producer Rayco Cano, his team and five big wave riders embrace us in their story about these huge waves. The film features windsurfing pro Daniel Bruch, German big wave surfer Alexander Zirke, Manuel Lezcano, Ayoze Fernandea and David Rodal Santiago Vilayta.San Borondón stands for big wave surfing and adrenalin driven surfers, who risk a lot on their surfing boards and SUPs. The jet ski is a necessary safety tool. Discover hidden places with beautiful nature in the Canary Islands as this team of adventurers dive into action on the water, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 3
programme.desc: In today's episode of The Boat Show we will dig deeper into our technical analysis of the Ranieri International Next 370 SH before trying out a new boat, the Azimut S7, and also a new concept that builds boats using mostly carbon fiber. Finally, we try out the Nor-tech 392.

programme.title: Wind Masters - Episode 3: Jelsa, Stari Grad, Hvar, Šolta, Vis, Komiža
programme.desc: Wind Masters - Episode 3: Jelsa, Stari Grad, Hvar, Šolta, Vis, Komiža

programme.title: Wind Masters - Episode 4: Šibenik, Murter, NP Krka, Vodice
programme.desc: Wind Masters - Episode 4: Šibenik, Murter, NP Krka, Vodice

programme.title: OFQUI
programme.desc: The Isthmus of Ofqui, unknown for many and chosen as surf destination by few adventurers.The stretch of land that separates the Pacific Ocean's inland seas, is the only way to make it if you want to surf the shores of Golfo de Penas. Connecting the Taitao Peninsula with the Chilean mainland, Ofqui offers world class waves. This thaw surf film approaches the vast territory of the Patagonia in different ways, this time, centered around Ofqui.Pro surfers will face ice-cold waters in their expedition to surf deathly waves in Ofqui, one of the most unknown locations of the Patagonia and with a microclimate that maximises the upcoming waves.Join them in their adventure to Ofqui here, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Moana Waiwai
programme.desc: Moana Waiwai follows the Free Swim journey of wahine (woman) Ally Davey as she takes on 70km of open ocean around the stunning Coromandel coastline to bring awareness to the protection of New Zealand’s oceans.For over three days it does more than encapsulate stunning panoramas on the extraordinary scenario of New Zealand. It is also inspiring for people as we become aware of the natural beauty of the ocean so we can protect it before it is too late.This is an inspirational film, not an activist propaganda, that highlights exploration and the intrinsic benefits of getting wet and looking below the surface. In Moana Waiwai Ally Davey will champion endurance of her human spirit in this seemingly impossible task to achieve a higher goal: protection of the oceans.Be part of her adventure on Nautical Channel.

programme.desc: The popular legend claims San Borondón is an island that appears and disappears, normally under a heavy veil of fog. As adventurers departed on missions to be the first to locate it, not all returned from the depths of the ocean.Now, San Borondón is the name many local and international surfers have given to a dangerous wave spot on the Canary Islands. The film producer Rayco Cano, his team and five big wave riders embrace us in their story about these huge waves. The film features windsurfing pro Daniel Bruch, German big wave surfer Alexander Zirke, Manuel Lezcano, Ayoze Fernandea and David Rodal Santiago Vilayta.San Borondón stands for big wave surfing and adrenalin driven surfers, who risk a lot on their surfing boards and SUPs. The jet ski is a necessary safety tool. Discover hidden places with beautiful nature in the Canary Islands as this team of adventurers dive into action on the water, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 3
programme.desc: In today's episode of The Boat Show we will dig deeper into our technical analysis of the Ranieri International Next 370 SH before trying out a new boat, the Azimut S7, and also a new concept that builds boats using mostly carbon fiber. Finally, we try out the Nor-tech 392.

programme.title: OFQUI
programme.desc: The Isthmus of Ofqui, unknown for many and chosen as surf destination by few adventurers.The stretch of land that separates the Pacific Ocean's inland seas, is the only way to make it if you want to surf the shores of Golfo de Penas. Connecting the Taitao Peninsula with the Chilean mainland, Ofqui offers world class waves. This thaw surf film approaches the vast territory of the Patagonia in different ways, this time, centered around Ofqui.Pro surfers will face ice-cold waters in their expedition to surf deathly waves in Ofqui, one of the most unknown locations of the Patagonia and with a microclimate that maximises the upcoming waves.Join them in their adventure to Ofqui here, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Moana Waiwai
programme.desc: Moana Waiwai follows the Free Swim journey of wahine (woman) Ally Davey as she takes on 70km of open ocean around the stunning Coromandel coastline to bring awareness to the protection of New Zealand’s oceans.For over three days it does more than encapsulate stunning panoramas on the extraordinary scenario of New Zealand. It is also inspiring for people as we become aware of the natural beauty of the ocean so we can protect it before it is too late.This is an inspirational film, not an activist propaganda, that highlights exploration and the intrinsic benefits of getting wet and looking below the surface. In Moana Waiwai Ally Davey will champion endurance of her human spirit in this seemingly impossible task to achieve a higher goal: protection of the oceans.Be part of her adventure on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Surf Ski - Episode 1 - Perth
programme.desc: Welcome to Perth, Australia for the Surfski World Tour. The Perth Doctor is the second leg of the championship, and Jasper Mocké, the younger brother of four times Surfski World Series winner Dawid Mocke has already started strong in the Mandurrah Duel. Will he win again in Perth? Let's find out on Surf Ski!

programme.title: Surf Ski - Episode 2 - Mauritius
programme.desc: This time we are in Mauritius for the fifth leg of the Surf Ski World Tour. Athletes are getting hyped up for the Mauritius Ocean Classic, and many want to stop Jasper Mocké from winning as he has already won 3 of the 4 events disputed so far this season. Could Hank McGregor, winner of the Molokai Challenge, be his strongest challenger?

programme.title: Vilanculos Downwind
programme.desc: We are in Mozambique, the southern African paradise with the long Indian Ocean coastline to its side. Mozambique is not only filled with popular beaches and offshore marine parks, but also incredible windsurfing spots. On this occasion, we celebrate Vilanculos Downwind, a never before done 20 kilometer kitesurf race from the mainland of Mozambique to Bazaruto Island.Join the windsurfers that will journey in the tricky Indian Ocean and race to reach the island of Bazaruto. Departing from the city of Vilanculos, they head for a journey downwind like no other. Don’t miss this once-in-a-lifetime experience, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Tiree Wave Classic - 2017
programme.desc: Thanks to the famed Atlantic swells that roll along its coastline and despite being home to just 650 people, this watersports mecca attracts surfers and windsurfers from across the world. Tiree's wild coastline is battered by some of the UK's highest wind ratings, creating the perfect meteorological conditions to host the longest running windsurfing event in the world - the Tiree Wave Classic. It is time for the 2017 edition. Come join us!

programme.desc: Inside Sailing - Episode 9

programme.title: Sailing to the Games - Episode 5
programme.desc: Sailing to the Games - Episode 5

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 1 - Dubrovnik - Orebic
programme.desc: We begin our journey inside Sail-Ho at the most beautiful and magical old city: Dubrovnik. Home to many famous TV series due to its historical and beautiful landscape, we are ready to sail around the Elafiti islands and introduce you to the top quality wines of Pelješac peninsula.

programme.title: MAR
programme.desc: Surfing has ceased a long time ago to be considered a niche sport. As the mainstream industry discovers its potential for selling clothes, trips and a whole set of ‘lifestyle’ products, there are those who appreciate it for what it is in its purest form – a direct connection to mother nature.We accompany Big Wave surfer Alex Botelho and some of his friends to meet surfers who balance their love for the ocean and nature with a professional career in the sport. Mar is a tale of discovery, triumph and (almost) tragedy, but above all it is a life affirming pledge to positive vibes in and out of the line-up.Get ready to soak up a new conception of the world of surf as Alex Botelho unveils concepts unknown for the mainstream audience, available on Nautical Channel.Awards:Best Feature Documentary – Best Istanbul Film Festival 2021. Official Selections 2021: Ocean Film Festival World Tour, International Ocean Film Festival.

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Breaking Ice
programme.desc: Host Lucia Metzbauer meets the crazy athletes who brave sup-zero temperatures pursuing their love of nautical sport. The frigid Austrian Alps prove to be rife with nautical sport as Lucia goes racing with ice sailors, meets the 5x world record holder of Ice diving, and discovers the little known sport of underwater Ice hockey!

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Munich
programme.desc: Munich, Germany, close to the Alps, but far away from the sea. In Munich, instead of waiting for vacations, surfers started experimenting on the 12m, 39ft wave in the Eisbach in the middle of the city 30 years ago.This way, river surfing was born. Nowadays, the wave is surfed day and night, during all seasons, attracting surf legends and tourists. But now the city wants to shut down the wave!

programme.title: Wind and Waves Gran Canaria
programme.desc: Welcome to Gran Canaria! This island in the Canary Islands is roughly 100 kilometres away from Morocco despite belonging to Spain, and the mix of cultures is palpable in the whole archipelago as visitors from all over the world come to enjoy its subtropical climate and optimal conditions for nautical sports.In this occasion, Lucia Metzbauer lands in Gran Canaria, host of the PWA World Championships. The high speed winds from Pozo Izquierdo drives the attention of athletes, so get ready to find out why this destination is one of the most attractive for windsurfing aficionados from around the world.Get on board in Wind and Waves Gran Canaria, a new adventure on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: San Borondón
programme.desc: The popular legend claims San Borondón is an island that appears and disappears, normally under a heavy veil of fog. As adventurers departed on missions to be the first to locate it, not all returned from the depths of the ocean.Now, San Borondón is the name many local and international surfers have given to a dangerous wave spot on the Canary Islands. The film producer Rayco Cano, his team and five big wave riders embrace us in their story about these huge waves. The film features windsurfing pro Daniel Bruch, German big wave surfer Alexander Zirke, Manuel Lezcano, Ayoze Fernandea and David Rodal Santiago Vilayta.San Borondón stands for big wave surfing and adrenalin driven surfers, who risk a lot on their surfing boards and SUPs. The jet ski is a necessary safety tool. Discover hidden places with beautiful nature in the Canary Islands as this team of adventurers dive into action on the water, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Tahitian Swell
programme.desc: The French Polynesia is world famous not only for its clear blue waters, but also for the quality of its waves to the delight of worldwide surfers. These waves are generated by the swell; an undulating movement of the sea surface that advances as it does not break.If the French Polynesia is one of the kingdoms of surfing, it is thanks to its geographical location. Nestled in the heart of the South Pacific, this group of five archipelagos cover an area as vast as Europe and benefit all year round from swells generated by the four winds.The conditions are therefore ideal for the many wave lovers: surfers, windsurfers, bodyboarders... but in order for them to enjoy these conditions, knowledge of marine forecasting is essential. This is where the Polynesian branch of Météo France takes place, including amongst their team swell specialists as Kenji Flohr, a Polynesian forecaster and surfer.His mission is to anticipate the waves that will break on the Polynesian coasts, by analysing the swells, their direction, period, energy, length, and dimensions. The expertise of this surfer forecaster enables to popularize a set of advanced analyses. This makes this didactic documentary accessible to all. And thanks to Kenji Flohr’s crystal clear explanations like the water of the Pacific Ocean, this complex system becomes clear and above all… fascinating.Learn more about the Tahitian Swell and the scientific approach for predicting waves, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Mr. Price Pro Ballito
programme.desc: Follow the world leaders of surfing as they come to South Africa, Willard beach Ballito. 96 competitors from 18 countries will surf for the 250,000$ prize. Also, music concerts filled with South African bands will be cheering the best surfers of the world with their jamming.

programme.title: Magnifique Martinique
programme.desc: Martinique is truly magnificent!The Caribbean Island is a paradise for both locals and visitors that year after year come to discover the mysteries of this island. Come discover the beauty of life in the Caribbean in this show.Nautical Channel’s host, Andrew Miller, will show you exciting new places and delicious local cuisine prepared by a great chef. Famous Caribbean Rum for breakfast and an intense session of fly boarding will guarantee madness fun in this exhilarating adventure in Magnifique Martinique.Follow Andrew on a unique discovery in this French holiday destination, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Maya Gabeira - Return to Nazaré
programme.desc: After a horrible crash in Nazaré that almost took her life, big wave surfer Maya Gabeira fights to overcome physical and mental obstacles and return to surf the giant waves again.Maya Gabeira, the Brazilian surfer that was close to death in 2013 in Nazaré due to big waves. She had to be rescued by lifeguards who struggled to get her out of waves that didn’t stop coming. After recovering physically and emotionally of the shock, she's ready to return to Nazaré to overcome the same obstacles that almost took her life years ago.Her determination is undeniable, as is the danger of tempting fate twice. However, once in Nazaré, will she have the strength needed to surf deathly waves?Follow her inspiring story in Maya Gabeira – Return to Nazaré.

programme.title: P1 USA AquaX - Episode 6 - Bahamas Second Part

programme.title: Sailing to the Games - Episode 5
programme.desc: Sailing to the Games - Episode 5

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 1 - Dubrovnik - Orebic
programme.desc: We begin our journey inside Sail-Ho at the most beautiful and magical old city: Dubrovnik. Home to many famous TV series due to its historical and beautiful landscape, we are ready to sail around the Elafiti islands and introduce you to the top quality wines of Pelješac peninsula.

programme.title: Magnifique Martinique
programme.desc: Martinique is truly magnificent!The Caribbean Island is a paradise for both locals and visitors that year after year come to discover the mysteries of this island. Come discover the beauty of life in the Caribbean in this show.Nautical Channel’s host, Andrew Miller, will show you exciting new places and delicious local cuisine prepared by a great chef. Famous Caribbean Rum for breakfast and an intense session of fly boarding will guarantee madness fun in this exhilarating adventure in Magnifique Martinique.Follow Andrew on a unique discovery in this French holiday destination, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Vilanculos Downwind
programme.desc: We are in Mozambique, the southern African paradise with the long Indian Ocean coastline to its side. Mozambique is not only filled with popular beaches and offshore marine parks, but also incredible windsurfing spots. On this occasion, we celebrate Vilanculos Downwind, a never before done 20 kilometer kitesurf race from the mainland of Mozambique to Bazaruto Island.Join the windsurfers that will journey in the tricky Indian Ocean and race to reach the island of Bazaruto. Departing from the city of Vilanculos, they head for a journey downwind like no other. Don’t miss this once-in-a-lifetime experience, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Tiree Wave Classic - 2017
programme.desc: Thanks to the famed Atlantic swells that roll along its coastline and despite being home to just 650 people, this watersports mecca attracts surfers and windsurfers from across the world. Tiree's wild coastline is battered by some of the UK's highest wind ratings, creating the perfect meteorological conditions to host the longest running windsurfing event in the world - the Tiree Wave Classic. It is time for the 2017 edition. Come join us!

programme.title: Qatar Airways GKA Kite World Tour Magazines - Episode 4
programme.desc: Qatar Airways GKA Kite World Tour Magazines - Episode 4

programme.title: Tahiti Pearl Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: Tahiti Pearl Regatta - Episode 1

programme.title: OFQUI
programme.desc: The Isthmus of Ofqui, unknown for many and chosen as surf destination by few adventurers.The stretch of land that separates the Pacific Ocean's inland seas, is the only way to make it if you want to surf the shores of Golfo de Penas. Connecting the Taitao Peninsula with the Chilean mainland, Ofqui offers world class waves. This thaw surf film approaches the vast territory of the Patagonia in different ways, this time, centered around Ofqui.Pro surfers will face ice-cold waters in their expedition to surf deathly waves in Ofqui, one of the most unknown locations of the Patagonia and with a microclimate that maximises the upcoming waves.Join them in their adventure to Ofqui here, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Moana Waiwai
programme.desc: Moana Waiwai follows the Free Swim journey of wahine (woman) Ally Davey as she takes on 70km of open ocean around the stunning Coromandel coastline to bring awareness to the protection of New Zealand’s oceans.For over three days it does more than encapsulate stunning panoramas on the extraordinary scenario of New Zealand. It is also inspiring for people as we become aware of the natural beauty of the ocean so we can protect it before it is too late.This is an inspirational film, not an activist propaganda, that highlights exploration and the intrinsic benefits of getting wet and looking below the surface. In Moana Waiwai Ally Davey will champion endurance of her human spirit in this seemingly impossible task to achieve a higher goal: protection of the oceans.Be part of her adventure on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Kite Masters - Episode 5
programme.desc: Kite Masters - Episode 5

programme.title: Kite Masters - Episode 6
programme.desc: Kite Masters - Episode 6

programme.title: San Borondón
programme.desc: The popular legend claims San Borondón is an island that appears and disappears, normally under a heavy veil of fog. As adventurers departed on missions to be the first to locate it, not all returned from the depths of the ocean.Now, San Borondón is the name many local and international surfers have given to a dangerous wave spot on the Canary Islands. The film producer Rayco Cano, his team and five big wave riders embrace us in their story about these huge waves. The film features windsurfing pro Daniel Bruch, German big wave surfer Alexander Zirke, Manuel Lezcano, Ayoze Fernandea and David Rodal Santiago Vilayta.San Borondón stands for big wave surfing and adrenalin driven surfers, who risk a lot on their surfing boards and SUPs. The jet ski is a necessary safety tool. Discover hidden places with beautiful nature in the Canary Islands as this team of adventurers dive into action on the water, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 3
programme.desc: In today's episode of The Boat Show we will dig deeper into our technical analysis of the Ranieri International Next 370 SH before trying out a new boat, the Azimut S7, and also a new concept that builds boats using mostly carbon fiber. Finally, we try out the Nor-tech 392.

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Breaking Ice
programme.desc: Host Lucia Metzbauer meets the crazy athletes who brave sup-zero temperatures pursuing their love of nautical sport. The frigid Austrian Alps prove to be rife with nautical sport as Lucia goes racing with ice sailors, meets the 5x world record holder of Ice diving, and discovers the little known sport of underwater Ice hockey!

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Munich
programme.desc: Munich, Germany, close to the Alps, but far away from the sea. In Munich, instead of waiting for vacations, surfers started experimenting on the 12m, 39ft wave in the Eisbach in the middle of the city 30 years ago.This way, river surfing was born. Nowadays, the wave is surfed day and night, during all seasons, attracting surf legends and tourists. But now the city wants to shut down the wave!

programme.title: Magnifique Martinique
programme.desc: Martinique is truly magnificent!The Caribbean Island is a paradise for both locals and visitors that year after year come to discover the mysteries of this island. Come discover the beauty of life in the Caribbean in this show.Nautical Channel’s host, Andrew Miller, will show you exciting new places and delicious local cuisine prepared by a great chef. Famous Caribbean Rum for breakfast and an intense session of fly boarding will guarantee madness fun in this exhilarating adventure in Magnifique Martinique.Follow Andrew on a unique discovery in this French holiday destination, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Perilous Sea
programme.desc: Perilous Sea is a transcontinental cold-water surfing odyssey capturing the raw spirit, glorious landscapes and pure perfection that arises when conditions align. This incredible story creates moments of cold-water nirvana that rival any across the globe.Inspired by the storytelling style of classic ocean-fairing novels, Perilous Sea follows a group of surfers as they explore the landscapes and waves of the North Atlantic fringes. From the Canadian Maritimes, north to Iceland and beyond to the wave rich coast of Ireland, this award-winning film takes us on a journey to the world of surf.Follow them into the water as they dare to get into the treacherous waters that have taken the lives of many in Perilous Sea: a story of overcoming challenges and spirituality.

programme.title: Eurosaf Championship Tavira
programme.desc: The EUROSAF (European Sailing Federation) Championship will take place in the waters off the Portuguese town of Tavira. The 19 sailing champions of Europe will be crowned in this gathering that has over 150 sailors and more than 100 boats and boards compete against triumph. 14 European countries have sent their best sailors to compete in eight different disciplines, including single-handed and double-handed dinghies, plus sailboards and high performance dinghy. They will compete over the 5-day competition to choose the youth champion of Europe in each of these disciplines. Follow the competition of the future talent of the sailing world as they sail across the waters of Tavira, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Tiree Wave Classic - 2018
programme.desc: In its 32nd year, the event continues to showcase the best, most extreme, windsurfing seen in competition. Attracting some of the best riders in the world to make the pilgrimage to the small island located in the inner Hebrides off the Northwest coast of Scotland, this is one of the four events taht make up this year's British Wave Sailing Tour Championship. Enjoy the Tiree Wave Classic from 2018, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: GWA Wingfoil World Tour - Episode 1
programme.desc: GWA Wingfoil World Tour - Episode 1

programme.title: South to Sian
programme.desc: In a time where there are fences around everything, and we are denied the instinct of self-preservation, it is difficult to find a place free from rules and restrictions, but not yet impossible.Surf movies come and go, a million waves in exotic locations and surfers flown in for three-day shoots on perfect swells, but the spirit of adventure never dies.What began as a three-month trip to a collection of surf breaks off the beaten track turned into a two-year odyssey of exploration, injury, companionship and 4,000km of two-wheeled, single-finned escape from the the real-world burdens of modern life.Harrison Roach and Zye Norris pack their bags, a diverse quiver of boards, two bikes and a 50-dollar tent into a 1970s Land Rover and embark on an epic quest from the southern reaches of Bali, through the Indonesian archipelago to Northern Sumatra’s isolated Lagundri Bay. On boards, on bikes, by boat and four-wheel drive, the pair’s journey is to discover if, in these days of GPS and WiFi, the dream of a true pioneering surf adventure is still alive.

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 3
programme.desc: This time we are going to focus on another exciting Olympic class, the RS:X. The RS:X was first introduced at the 2008 Olympic Games in China. Being the most affordable yachting class eligible for competition in the Olympics, it has been taken up by a large number of sailors from more than 60 nations over 6 continents competing at numerous regattas worldwide.Stay tuned for the next episode as we follow the sailing action around the world! The final countdown for the Olympics in Tokyo has only just begun!

programme.title: Heineken Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: Boasting world class competition and legendary parties, the St. Maarten Heineken Regatta reigns as the undisputed king of Caribbean sailing. Given the lineup of over 200 internationally acclaimed boats skippered by the sailing world's top athletes, the stakes are high and anything can happen.Sail with us across the highlights of the 34th edition of the Heineken Regatta!

programme.title: RKJ Sailing Legend
programme.desc: Born in Putney, London, Sir William Robert Patrick Knox-Johnston served in the Merchant Navy, where he acquired his skills to later on become a British legend and be awarded the title of Sir.RKJ Sailing Legend is a 56-minute documentary about possibly the most famous sailor in modern times, Sir Robin Knox-Johnston. RKJ was the first man to sail non-stop and alone around the world back in 1969: Now on the 50th anniversary of his achievement, we want to rememorate his feat.Get on board RKJ Sailing Legend, the remarkable story of Knox-Johnston's voyage around the world and into history. Available on Nautical Channel.

Being the most affordable yachting class eligible for competition in the Olympics, it has been taken up by a large number of sailors from more than 60 nations over 6 continents competing at numerous regattas worldwide.Stay tuned for the next episode as we follow the sailing action around the world! The final countdown for the Olympics in Tokyo has only just begun!

programme.title: Heineken Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: Boasting world class competition and legendary parties, the St. Maarten Heineken Regatta reigns as the undisputed king of Caribbean sailing. Given the lineup of over 200 internationally acclaimed boats skippered by the sailing world's top athletes, the stakes are high and anything can happen.Sail with us across the highlights of the 34th edition of the Heineken Regatta!

programme.title: Tiree Wave Classic - 2018
programme.desc: In its 32nd year, the event continues to showcase the best, most extreme, windsurfing seen in competition. Attracting some of the best riders in the world to make the pilgrimage to the small island located in the inner Hebrides off the Northwest coast of Scotland, this is one of the four events taht make up this year's British Wave Sailing Tour Championship. Enjoy the Tiree Wave Classic from 2018, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: GWA Wingfoil World Tour - Episode 1
programme.desc: GWA Wingfoil World Tour - Episode 1

programme.title: San Borondón
programme.desc: The popular legend claims San Borondón is an island that appears and disappears, normally under a heavy veil of fog. As adventurers departed on missions to be the first to locate it, not all returned from the depths of the ocean.Now, San Borondón is the name many local and international surfers have given to a dangerous wave spot on the Canary Islands. The film producer Rayco Cano, his team and five big wave riders embrace us in their story about these huge waves. The film features windsurfing pro Daniel Bruch, German big wave surfer Alexander Zirke, Manuel Lezcano, Ayoze Fernandea and David Rodal Santiago Vilayta.San Borondón stands for big wave surfing and adrenalin driven surfers, who risk a lot on their surfing boards and SUPs. The jet ski is a necessary safety tool. Discover hidden places with beautiful nature in the Canary Islands as this team of adventurers dive into action on the water, available on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 3
programme.desc: In today's episode of The Boat Show we will dig deeper into our technical analysis of the Ranieri International Next 370 SH before trying out a new boat, the Azimut S7, and also a new concept that builds boats using mostly carbon fiber. Finally, we try out the Nor-tech 392.

programme.title: OFQUI
programme.desc: The Isthmus of Ofqui, unknown for many and chosen as surf destination by few adventurers.The stretch of land that separates the Pacific Ocean's inland seas, is the only way to make it if you want to surf the shores of Golfo de Penas. Connecting the Taitao Peninsula with the Chilean mainland, Ofqui offers world class waves. This thaw surf film approaches the vast territory of the Patagonia in different ways, this time, centered around Ofqui.Pro surfers will face ice-cold waters in their expedition to surf deathly waves in Ofqui, one of the most unknown locations of the Patagonia and with a microclimate that maximises the upcoming waves.Join them in their adventure to Ofqui here, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Moana Waiwai
programme.desc: Moana Waiwai follows the Free Swim journey of wahine (woman) Ally Davey as she takes on 70km of open ocean around the stunning Coromandel coastline to bring awareness to the protection of New Zealand’s oceans.For over three days it does more than encapsulate stunning panoramas on the extraordinary scenario of New Zealand. It is also inspiring for people as we become aware of the natural beauty of the ocean so we can protect it before it is too late.This is an inspirational film, not an activist propaganda, that highlights exploration and the intrinsic benefits of getting wet and looking below the surface. In Moana Waiwai Ally Davey will champion endurance of her human spirit in this seemingly impossible task to achieve a higher goal: protection of the oceans.Be part of her adventure on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Breaking Ice
programme.desc: Host Lucia Metzbauer meets the crazy athletes who brave sup-zero temperatures pursuing their love of nautical sport. The frigid Austrian Alps prove to be rife with nautical sport as Lucia goes racing with ice sailors, meets the 5x world record holder of Ice diving, and discovers the little known sport of underwater Ice hockey!

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Munich
programme.desc: Munich, Germany, close to the Alps, but far away from the sea. In Munich, instead of waiting for vacations, surfers started experimenting on the 12m, 39ft wave in the Eisbach in the middle of the city 30 years ago.This way, river surfing was born. Nowadays, the wave is surfed day and night, during all seasons, attracting surf legends and tourists. But now the city wants to shut down the wave!

programme.title: Magnifique Martinique
programme.desc: Martinique is truly magnificent!The Caribbean Island is a paradise for both locals and visitors that year after year come to discover the mysteries of this island. Come discover the beauty of life in the Caribbean in this show.Nautical Channel’s host, Andrew Miller, will show you exciting new places and delicious local cuisine prepared by a great chef. Famous Caribbean Rum for breakfast and an intense session of fly boarding will guarantee madness fun in this exhilarating adventure in Magnifique Martinique.Follow Andrew on a unique discovery in this French holiday destination, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: The Cyclades With Andi Van Zyl - Ios
programme.desc: Andi van Zyl is in Ios Island at the time the Meltemi winds start, and she is ready to enjoy watersports both above and below the surface. Andi will understand the significance this island has on its habitants when she discovers what is believed to be the tomb of Homer: the creator of the Iliad and the Odyssey. This is our final stop in this journey around the Cyclades, so Andi is ready to make the most of it.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 4
programme.desc: We are in Lake Iseo, and it is said on these waters the first motor boat was operated. Now, we will be trying the Ecoline Marine 800 Hybrid that works on diesel propulsion and an electric motor. Then, we will try the Della Pasqua DC Seven, produced by the Italian yard Della Pasqua. Come sail with us!

programme.title: Wind Masters - Episode 5: Biograd, Sukošan, Bibinje, Ugljan, Pašman, Dugi Otok
programme.desc: Wind Masters - Episode 5: Biograd, Sukošan, Bibinje, Ugljan, Pašman, Dugi Otok

programme.title: Wind Masters - Episode 6: Zadar, Nin, Iž, Olib, Silba, Premuda, Molat, Pag
programme.desc: Wind Masters - Episode 6: Zadar, Nin, Iž, Olib, Silba, Premuda, Molat, Pag

programme.title: Beneath the Surface
programme.desc: Big wave surfer Andrew Cotton, a plumber and father of two from Devon, hit the headlines in 2014 when it was rumoured that he had surfed the world’s biggest wave, estimated at 80ft. In this one-off 30-minute documentary, Andrew and his team of surfers, sailors and adventurers put everything on hold as they secretly chase a wave that emerges only a handful of times each year, giving only hours of warning.Taking a boat eight miles off the coast of Ireland, the team battle the extreme cold and fierce weather conditions, whilst mapping the ocean’s swell, in order to try to catch one almighty ride. Far from home and with the days getting shorter, frustration sets in, as the window of opportunity gets smaller. With a young family at home, Andrew must balance the normality of the school run, before boarding a plane alone to face one of earth’s deadliest monsters.

programme.title: The Road To Bermuda - Episode 1
programme.desc: In this first episode of Road to Bermuda we meet the man who decided to take the America's Cup to Bermuda and explore some of sailing's most complex challenges. Also, the famous skipper Jimmy Spithill will share his experience on the island with us. Watch this and more on The Road to Bermuda!

programme.title: Jump On Board - Episode 5
programme.desc: In this episode of Jump on Board, Cindy, Caitlin and Linda sail from Ithaca to Cephalonia, the largest of the Ionian Islands in Western Greece and home to our captain, Marcos. The girls get divided like the semi sweet water that runs throughout the island's caves - half sweet, half salt. In the end, it's Marcos who gets the largest dose of sweet from one of the ladies. Curious to see how it goes? Jump on board with us to find out!

programme.title: Jump On Board - Episode 6
programme.desc: This time, Caitlin, Cindy and Linda sail from Cephalonia to Zante, called by the Venetians, "The flower of the East." The girls will explore a secluded beach amidst white steep cliffs so surreal that it might as well be on Venus, not Greece. Will the crew begin to get sentimental now that the trip is coming to a close?

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 2 - Brac – Hvar
programme.desc: In this episode we are taking you to a surfer's paradise – the island of Brač! We are on a journey to find out why Brač is called by many the island of a million olives, and we are going to explore the beautiful Pakleni islands that are close to this location. Get to know the fantastic wines of the island of Hvar and treat ourselves to some drunk lobster.

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 3 - Split – Trogir
programme.desc: It is time to explore the wonders of Split, city of the monumental Diocletian's Palace. In this occasion, we will introduce you to the region's beautiful marinas, show you the fastest way of traveling within Croatia and explore the spots you should not miss out.

programme.title: Second Life In Scandinavia
programme.desc: Hello from Sweden!We are on our way to the island of Orust. This island lives from boatbuilding, and big companies produce sailing boats here to expand their boats to the entire world. When the economic crisis stroke, demand decreased, thus less boats were ordered. This forced habitants of the small island of Orust to find a new path.Refit has become the new trend in Second Life in Scandinavia. Giving a new life to beautiful yachts, not only the economy is changing, but the local community as well. Lucia will venture into the island of Orust to learn more about the way its population has adapted and how it has become the mecca of boatbuilding.Watch out now, on Nautical Channel.

programme.desc: Martinique is truly magnificent!The Caribbean Island is a paradise for both locals and visitors that year after year come to discover the mysteries of this island. Come discover the beauty of life in the Caribbean in this show.Nautical Channel’s host, Andrew Miller, will show you exciting new places and delicious local cuisine prepared by a great chef. Famous Caribbean Rum for breakfast and an intense session of fly boarding will guarantee madness fun in this exhilarating adventure in Magnifique Martinique.Follow Andrew on a unique discovery in this French holiday destination, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 2 - Brac – Hvar
programme.desc: In this episode we are taking you to a surfer's paradise – the island of Brač! We are on a journey to find out why Brač is called by many the island of a million olives, and we are going to explore the beautiful Pakleni islands that are close to this location. Get to know the fantastic wines of the island of Hvar and treat ourselves to some drunk lobster.

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 3 - Split – Trogir
programme.desc: It is time to explore the wonders of Split, city of the monumental Diocletian's Palace. In this occasion, we will introduce you to the region's beautiful marinas, show you the fastest way of traveling within Croatia and explore the spots you should not miss out.

programme.title: Beneath the Surface
programme.desc: Big wave surfer Andrew Cotton, a plumber and father of two from Devon, hit the headlines in 2014 when it was rumoured that he had surfed the world’s biggest wave, estimated at 80ft. In this one-off 30-minute documentary, Andrew and his team of surfers, sailors and adventurers put everything on hold as they secretly chase a wave that emerges only a handful of times each year, giving only hours of warning.Taking a boat eight miles off the coast of Ireland, the team battle the extreme cold and fierce weather conditions, whilst mapping the ocean’s swell, in order to try to catch one almighty ride. Far from home and with the days getting shorter, frustration sets in, as the window of opportunity gets smaller. With a young family at home, Andrew must balance the normality of the school run, before boarding a plane alone to face one of earth’s deadliest monsters.

programme.title: The Road To Bermuda - Episode 1
programme.desc: In this first episode of Road to Bermuda we meet the man who decided to take the America's Cup to Bermuda and explore some of sailing's most complex challenges. Also, the famous skipper Jimmy Spithill will share his experience on the island with us. Watch this and more on The Road to Bermuda!

programme.title: Kite Masters - Episode 5
programme.desc: Kite Masters - Episode 5

programme.title: Kite Masters - Episode 6
programme.desc: Kite Masters - Episode 6

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Breaking Ice
programme.desc: Host Lucia Metzbauer meets the crazy athletes who brave sup-zero temperatures pursuing their love of nautical sport. The frigid Austrian Alps prove to be rife with nautical sport as Lucia goes racing with ice sailors, meets the 5x world record holder of Ice diving, and discovers the little known sport of underwater Ice hockey!

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Munich
programme.desc: Munich, Germany, close to the Alps, but far away from the sea. In Munich, instead of waiting for vacations, surfers started experimenting on the 12m, 39ft wave in the Eisbach in the middle of the city 30 years ago.This way, river surfing was born. Nowadays, the wave is surfed day and night, during all seasons, attracting surf legends and tourists. But now the city wants to shut down the wave!

programme.title: Tiree Wave Classic - 2018
programme.desc: In its 32nd year, the event continues to showcase the best, most extreme, windsurfing seen in competition. Attracting some of the best riders in the world to make the pilgrimage to the small island located in the inner Hebrides off the Northwest coast of Scotland, this is one of the four events taht make up this year's British Wave Sailing Tour Championship. Enjoy the Tiree Wave Classic from 2018, on Nautical Channel.

programme.title: GWA Wingfoil World Tour - Episode 1
programme.desc: GWA Wingfoil World Tour - Episode 1

programme.title: Sailing To Tokyo - Episode 3
programme.desc: This time we are going to focus on another exciting Olympic class, the RS:X. The RS:X was first introduced at the 2008 Olympic Games in China. Being the most affordable yachting class eligible for competition in the Olympics, it has been taken up by a large number of sailors from more than 60 nations over 6 continents competing at numerous regattas worldwide.Stay tuned for the next episode as we follow the sailing action around the world! The final countdown for the Olympics in Tokyo has only just begun!

programme.title: Heineken Regatta - Episode 1
programme.desc: Boasting world class competition and legendary parties, the St. Maarten Heineken Regatta reigns as the undisputed king of Caribbean sailing. Given the lineup of over 200 internationally acclaimed boats skippered by the sailing world's top athletes, the stakes are high and anything can happen.Sail with us across the highlights of the 34th edition of the Heineken Regatta!

programme.desc: Martinique is truly magnificent!The Caribbean Island is a paradise for both locals and visitors that year after year come to discover the mysteries of this island. programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 3 - Split – Trogir
programme.desc: It is time to explore the wonders of Split, city of the monumental Diocletian's Palace. In this occasion, we will introduce you to the region's beautiful marinas, show you the fastest way of traveling within Croatia and explore the spots you should not miss out.

programme.title: The Cyclades With Andi Van Zyl - Ios
programme.desc: Andi van Zyl is in Ios Island at the time the Meltemi winds start, and she is ready to enjoy watersports both above and below the surface. Andi will understand the significance this island has on its habitants when she discovers what is believed to be the tomb of Homer: the creator of the Iliad and the Odyssey. This is our final stop in this journey around the Cyclades, so Andi is ready to make the most of it.

programme.title: The Boat Show - Episode 4
programme.desc: We are in Lake Iseo, and it is said on these waters the first motor boat was operated. Now, we will be trying the Ecoline Marine 800 Hybrid that works on diesel propulsion and an electric motor. Then, we will try the Della Pasqua DC Seven, produced by the Italian yard Della Pasqua. Come sail with us!

programme.title: Sail-Ho - Episode 3 - Split – Trogir
programme.desc: It is time to explore the wonders of Split, city of the monumental Diocletian's Palace. In this occasion, we will introduce you to the region's beautiful marinas, show you the fastest way of traveling within Croatia and explore the spots you should not miss out.

programme.title: Iballa - Corazón de Escamas
programme.desc: After more than two decades in the world class of sports and overcoming different personal and sporting challenges of great significance, Iballa Ruano Moreno achieved in 2018 an historical landmark unprecedented in professional sports: winning the world championships in the same year the PWA World Circuit of Windsurf and the APP World Tour of Stand Up Paddle. This milestone never before achieved by an active athlete, highlights the level of dedication and improvement that the Moreno Twins and specifically Iballa Ruano, have been successfully reaping for so many years. However, the journey wasn’t easy. Corazón de Escamas is a short biographical documentary told in the first person and that shows the most intimate part of its history and is dedicated with great affection to the Ruano Moreno family. Dive into the world of this Canarian athlete and find out her inspiring story.

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Winter Kiting In St Moritz
programme.desc: The ice cold winter of Switzerland, wind, a board and a kite! Welcome to the adventure of winter kiting! Join us as we visit the oldest snowkiting school in the world and admire the action the snowkiting legends put up. All of this on the immense frozen Silvaplana Lake in the middle of the highest peaks of the Swiss Alps.

programme.title: Surf The Alps - Zurich Wakeboard
programme.desc: Despite being nestled in the heart of the Swiss Alps, Lake Zurich is home to a wealth of water sports. Journalist Lucia Metzbauer wakeboards with Swiss pros, attends the annual match race, and even tries stand-up paddleboarding before discovering a little-known sport: kanupolo.Also, the International Wakeboard Competition of Züri Fäscht 2013 is taking place in the centre of Zurich, and athletes are ready for Switzerland's greatest freestyle competition in this class. Let's dive into it!

programme.title: World Rowing Beach Sprint - Episode 1
programme.desc: This relatively new exciting format in Coastal Rowing held its first edition of the World Rowing Beach Sprint Finals in Shenzhen, China in 2019. Athletes raced in four boat classes: the mixed double sculls (CMix2x), mixed coxed quadruple sculls (CMix4x+) and men’s and women’s solo (CM1x & CW1x).Watch the best moments of the 2019 edition.

programme.title: World Rowing Coastal Championship - Episode 1
programme.desc: Competition has docked in Vancouver for the 2019 World Rowing Coastal Championship. An endurance test like no other, the event has buoy turns usually with a minimum distance of 4000m. With the stunning background of Vancouver Island, the World Champion of seven different boat classes will be crowned in this final.Watch the best moments of the 2019 competition of the World Rowing Coastal Championship.
